display.purpose: Hero title only. One element per page. `clamp` ensures the dramatic scale ratio holds at every viewport.

body.purpose: Primary supporting copy. The clamp scales with the viewport to maintain a 4-6x ratio with Display.

label.purpose: Button text, input placeholder, form labels. Fixed size; does not respond to viewport.

small.purpose: Error messages, helper text, fine print. Used sparingly.

shadows.name: ochre-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary button hover only. Tinted to match button color — glows, not drops. The only shadow in the system.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for all transitions and entrances. Confident deceleration.

motion.name: duration-feedback
motion.purpose: Instant feedback: button hover scale, press.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: State changes: border-color transitions, focus ring appearance.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.note: Email form switches from flex-col to flex-row at this breakpoint.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one interactive element that carries Sienna Ochre as a background. Used for the primary CTA (email sign-up).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(72% 0.10 62); color: oklch(18% 0.008 75); padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: scale(1.03); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px oklch(62% 0.14 58 / 0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(52% 0.16 48); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Email Input
components.description: The email capture field. Uses tonal layering (slightly lighter than page bg) for depth without shadow.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(97% 0.012 66); color: oklch(18% 0.008 75); border: 1.5px solid oklch(72% 0.035 66); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; width: 100%; outline: none; transition: border-color 200ms ease, box-shadow 200ms ease; } .ds-input:focus { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(52% 0.16 48), 0 0 0 4px oklch(93% 0.022 66); } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: oklch(50% 0.15 25); }

components.name: Email Capture Form
components.description: The inline sign-up form. Switches from stacked (mobile) to row (sm+). Animates out on valid submit; success message animates in.
components.css: .ds-email-form { display: flex; gap: 12px; flex-wrap: wrap; max-width: 448px; margin: 0 auto; } .ds-email-form__field { flex: 1; min-width: 200px; } .ds-input { background: oklch(97% 0.012 66); color: oklch(18% 0.008 75); border: 1.5px solid oklch(72% 0.035 66); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; width: 100%; outline: none; transition: border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-input:focus { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px oklch(52% 0.16 48), 0 0 0 4px oklch(93% 0.022 66); } .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(72% 0.10 62); color: oklch(18% 0.008 75); padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: scale(1.03); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px oklch(62% 0.14 58 / 0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Sign-up Success State
components.description: Replaces the form after valid submission. Rises in with fade-up animation. role=status for screen readers.
components.html: You’re on the list. We’ll be in touch.
components.css: @keyframes ds-fade-up { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(22px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } } .ds-signup-success { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.1rem; color: oklch(35% 0.012 70); margin: 0; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { .ds-signup-success { animation: ds-fade-up 0.7s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both; } }

components.name: Error Message
components.description: Inline validation error below the email input. Slides down on appearance. Cleared on any onChange event.
components.html: Enter a valid email address.
components.css: @keyframes ds-fade-down { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-5px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } } .ds-field-error { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(50% 0.15 25); margin-top: 6px; text-align: left; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { .ds-field-error { animation: ds-fade-down 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both; } }

narrative.overview:
This system is a place where real work gets done. The aesthetic reads like a well-lit studio: warm, amber-tinted surfaces, tools that feel honest in the hand, nothing decorative that doesn't earn its place. The mascot — a grinning chimp — is the heart of the brand. Every design decision either gives the mascot room to breathe or gets out of its way.

Typography does one thing dramatically. The display title arrives at clamp(3.5rem, 11vw, 7.5rem) — massive, tight-tracked, commanding the viewport before anything else loads. Everything beneath it drops to body scale and disappears into support. This ratio is the system's primary hierarchy mechanism. When in doubt, make the important thing bigger and everything else smaller.

Color is committed, not safe. Sienna Ochre — drawn directly from the mascot's fur — ties the illustration to the UI. It lives on the primary button, the focus ring, the scrollbar thumb, the animated hover shadow. It is the one accent voice.
keyCharacteristics: Mascot-led: the chimp is the primary brand signal; UI elements support it
One accent voice: Sienna Ochre everywhere interactive, nowhere decorative
Dramatic type hierarchy: 4-6x scale ratio between display and body
Flat surfaces, state-based shadows: nothing elevates at rest
Motion is purposeful: entrances, feedback, and state transitions only

rules.name: The One Ochre Rule
rules.body: Sienna Ochre and Burnt Ochre are the only saturated colors in the system. Use them only on interactive elements. Never on decorative elements, headings, or backgrounds.

rules.name: The Warm Tint Rule
rules.body: Every neutral must have chroma between 0.004 and 0.022, tinted toward hue 66-75. Pure grays and #000/#fff are forbidden.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Space Grotesk is the only typeface. Hierarchy comes from weight and scale, not variety.

rules.name: The Tracking Gate
rules.body: Display: -0.045em. Body: -0.01em. Label: 0em. Never positive letter-spacing on Space Grotesk.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No element carries elevation at rest. Shadows are responses to user intent (hover, focus), not passive decoration.

rules.name: The Tonal Depth Rule
rules.body: Depth between surfaces uses tonal layering (lighter OKLCH lightness value), not shadows. Shadows are reserved for interactive state.

dos: Do use Sienna Ochre (oklch(72% 0.10 62)) and Burnt Ochre (oklch(52% 0.16 48)) for all interactive states.
Do use Space Grotesk Bold only for the display title.
Do create hierarchy through extreme scale contrast: 4-6x ratio between display and body.
Do wrap all CSS animations in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference).
Do use cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for all UI transitions and entrances.
Do use OKLCH for all new colors, tinted toward hue 66-75.
Do give the mascot the dominant position on any screen it appears.
Do use tonal layering for depth. Shadows appear only on hover/focus state.
Do use role=alert for error messages and role=status for success states.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Absolute ban.
Don't use the purple primary color scale in tailwind.config.ts — it is vestigial from a previous design direction.
Don't use glassmorphism: blurred backdrop-filter cards or frosted surfaces.
Don't use generic SaaS patterns: floating feature grids, identical card grids, hero metric templates.
Don't use cluttered agency site patterns: competing scroll animations, multiple CTA buttons.
Don't apply border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored stripe accent.
Don't animate layout properties (width, height, top, left, margin). Transform and opacity only.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing curves.
Don't add a shadow to a surface at rest.
Don't introduce a second typeface. Add scale contrast instead.
Don't use #000 or #fff directly. The period in nomonkey.work is the sole exception.
